title: Execute — The Close
intro:
STEP 8 OF 8 · MODULE 8

Execute — The Close

Mission: Deliver commitment. Make the “yes” the path of least resistance.

OBJECTIVE
AUTHORIZED WORK
CORE MOVE
ASSUMPTIVE CLOSE
PRIMARY KPI
AUTHORIZATION RATE
FAILURE MODE
ASKING FOR PERMISSION

sections.title: The Closing Principle — Closing Starts at Hello
sections.html:
The Closing Principle — Closing Starts at Hello

The biggest myth in service writing is that “the close” happens at the end. It doesn’t. The close is the cumulative result of seven correctly executed prior steps. By the time the advisor reaches Step 8, the close is already either won or lost. Step 8 is about not breaking it.

Customers don’t follow conviction. Customers follow certainty.
Your job is to be the most certain person in the conversation.

sections.title: The Momentum Funnel
sections.html:
The Momentum Funnel

Figure 8.1 · Trust flows down. By the funnel mouth, the close is gravity.

sections.title: The Three Families of Close
sections.html:
The Three Families of Close

CLOSE FAMILY 1

Assumptive

Skip the decision. Move to logistics. “I’ll go ahead and get the parts ordered.” “While your car is here, we’ll…” The customer would have to stop motion to say no — far harder than just saying yes.

CLOSE FAMILY 2

Choice (Binary)

Collapse infinity to two options. “Would you prefer option A or option B?” “Should we pick it up at 9:15 or 10:45?” The binary close is the East-West applied to any decision.

CLOSE FAMILY 3

Logistical

The legendary “which direction would you like your vehicle facing when we bring it up?” The brain has to engage with parking, not price. By the time it’s engaged, the price is silently accepted.

PSYCHOLOGY DECODERWhy “which direction facing?” is the most quietly powerful close in the book.

The brain processes “which direction?” as a logistics problem. Logistics problems are answered on autopilot — the way the brain answers “cream and sugar?” By engaging with logistics, the customer’s subconscious has just confirmed the transaction is complete. There is no longer anything to negotiate. The price has been silently accepted.

sections.title: Home Plate — The Final Review
sections.html:
Home Plate — The Final Review

Home Plate is not the end of the sale — it’s the start of the next one. Done well, it’s where the customer becomes a repeat customer for life.

SCRIPTHome Plate · Final Invoice Review

“(Mr./Mrs. Customer), thank you again for trusting us with your vehicle today. Here’s what we completed: [services]. Everything is digitally documented in your invoice. Your total investment today is $____. Which direction would you like your vehicle facing when we bring it up?

Before you head out, a few benefits included with today’s service: 3-year / 36,000-mile nationwide warranty, 365-day roadside assistance, post-repair inspection, maintenance reminders, lifetime protection enrollment if applicable. We just want vehicle ownership to feel easy and predictable. We appreciate your business and we’ll be here whenever you need us.”

sections.title: Mastery Check
sections.html:
Mastery Check

LEVEL 8 · CLOSE MASTERY

  • Used at least one assumptive close line in every workflow.
  • Investment stated as a single number; warranty followed immediately, no pause.
  • Closed Home Plate with “which direction facing?”
  • Re-enrolled customer in lifetime protection or maintenance plan where applicable.
  • Asked for the next visit / review before customer left the lobby.
Authorization Rate
≥ 75%
Authorized $ ÷ presented $ across all bases.
ARO (Avg Repair Order)
Shop benchmark
Track per-advisor. Variance flags coaching opportunity.
5-Star Review %
≥ 80%
Customers asked at Home Plate for a review — conversion to 5-star.
